rag_rules: When fixing AGREE diagnostics, resolve type errors by ensuring all identifiers and expressions have valid, visible types in the current scope.
If a reference cannot be resolved, trace the root cause to an invalid helper or feature declaration and correct or remove it.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always ensure that expression types match expected scalar types (int, real, bool) and that references are visible in the current scope.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types that are not declared in the model or its dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid annex scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for assignments.
Guarantee expressions must evaluate to boolean types; use valid feature or helper declarations with matching types.
anti_patterns: Do not add new features or types to the model when fixing AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage
